data.title: chore: replace stale ~/.claude/skills and legacy path assumptions in shared skills
data.description:
## Problem

Several moved skills still instruct agents to read or write from legacy locations even though the review staging area is now repo-local `skills/`.

Concrete examples:

* workflows under `skills/skogai-routing/workflows/` still use `~/.claude/skills/...`
* matching workflows under `skills/skogai-skills/workflows/` also use `~/.claude/skills/...`
* `skills/skogai-docs/SKILL.md` still references `.claude/skills/skill-creator/scripts/init_skill.py`
* some moved skills still mention unavailable or no-longer-canonical interaction primitives such as `AskUserQuestion`

## Scope

* replace stale path instructions with current repo-local paths or clearly documented compatibility shims
* audit moved skills for legacy runtime assumptions and mark which are intentionally Claude-only vs portable
* remove or rewrite interaction patterns that do not exist in the current harness
* document the canonical relationship between repo-local `skills/`, any loaded plugin skills, and the longer-term registry direction

## Done when

* repo-local review workflows no longer point at `~/.claude/skills/` by default
* stale `.claude/skills/...` references are removed or intentionally documented as compatibility paths
* unavailable interaction primitives are not presented as normal workflow steps
* a reviewer can follow the moved skills from this repo without silently leaving the repo context
